q: Fluid is a substance that offers no permanent resistance to deforming forces.
Fluid statics concentrates on fluids in motion.
Hydrodynamics is the study of liquid at rest.
The volume of a gas depends on pressure and temperature.
The density of a liquid is constant for any pressure provided the temperature is kept constant.
The path or flow of fluid particles is called a streamline.
In laminar flow, streamlines cross each other frequently.
In steady laminar flow, every fluid particle arriving at a given point has the same velocity.
Turbulent flow is characterized by smooth paths in layers.
In turbulent flow, the speed of the fluid at a point is continuously undergoing changes in both magnitude and direction.
Internal friction in fluids is called viscosity.
Ideal laminar fluid flow assumes the fluid is compressible.
Ideal laminar fluid flow ignores or neglects friction.
